items.prompt: An array of length n receives 200,000 operations of the form “add delta to every index in inclusive [l, r]”. Only the final array is needed after all operations. Which representation is the decisive prefix-family choice?
constraints: No intermediate query must be answered.

options.text: Record a +delta change at l and a -delta change after r, then prefix-accumulate all changes once.
Immediately visit every index in [l, r] for each update.
Sort the array values before applying the updates.
Maintain one current window sum and move it between update ranges.

feedback.reason: The final effect changes only when an update becomes active at l and stops after r, so endpoints are sufficient until one reconstruction pass.
feedback.details: A difference array stores changes between adjacent final values rather than materializing every update immediately. Marking diff[l] += delta and diff[r + 1] -= delta causes the running prefix of diff to equal the active accumulated update at each index. This trades O(length of range) per update for O(1) marks and one O(n) pass.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.apply_every_element: Direct application is correct but can cost O(n) per update, producing O(un) work in the worst case. It ignores the endpoint cancellation structure.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.sort_update_ranges: The updates are positional. Sorting values changes which indices each range refers to and does not reduce the range-write cost.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.moving_window_sum: A window represents one active interval while scanning; it does not encode many overlapping future additions to reconstruct later.

items.prompt: After all endpoint marks are written, what does the running prefix of diff represent at index i?
constraints: Each update [l, r] adds +delta at l and -delta immediately after r when that boundary exists.

options.text: The total delta from all updates whose ranges include index i.
The original A[i] even before it is added to the accumulated delta.
The number of indices in one current moving window.
The number of update deltas smaller than A[i].

feedback.reason: Prefixing the boundary changes turns start and stop events into the cumulative update active at each position.
feedback.details: Every +delta remains in the running total from l through r, and the -delta after r removes it before the next index. Overlapping updates add naturally because their active intervals contribute simultaneously. The base array is separate: final[i] = A[i] + activeDelta[i] unless diff was initialized from A itself.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.final_value_without_base: The accumulated difference marks describe updates, not the original element unless the representation explicitly included A’s own differences.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.current_window_length: The running value is a sum of deltas, not a boundary distance. Many update intervals can be active at once.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.sorted_update_count: No sorting or value comparison is involved. The state is determined only by positional start and stop events.

items.prompt: Order the operations for applying a batch of inclusive range-add updates with a difference array.
constraints: The base array A is retained separately.
Score is based on preserved adjacent relations.

elements.text: Initialize diff with zeros for all required boundaries.
For every update, add delta at l and subtract delta at r + 1 when that boundary exists.
Scan left to right, accumulating diff into the active delta.
Add the active delta to A[i] to produce the final value at i.
Emit the completed final array after the reconstruction scan.

feedback.reason: All endpoint events must be recorded before one cumulative reconstruction converts them into per-index effects.
feedback.details: Writing all marks first keeps every update O(1). The reconstruction scan then integrates the changes in index order and combines the active total with the base value. Applying a base value before its active delta is known or emitting before all marks are recorded breaks the deferred-update contract.

items.prompt: For an inclusive update [l, n - 1], how should the stop mark be handled in a length-n diff array?
constraints: diff has indices 0 through n - 1 only.
The reconstruction scan ends at n - 1.

options.text: Write diff[l] += delta and omit the stop mark because r + 1 equals n, outside the reconstructed domain.
Write diff[n - 1] -= delta so the last element is not updated.
Write the stop mark at diff[0].
Sort diff and place the negative mark at its last value.

feedback.reason: An update that reaches the final index never needs to stop within the scanned domain.
feedback.details: The +delta must remain active through n - 1. A stop at r would cancel it before the last element, while wrapping to zero would corrupt the whole prefix. An alternative legal representation allocates n + 1 boundaries and writes the stop at diff[n], but a length-n array must guard the write.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.subtract_at_r: The stop boundary is after r, not at r. Subtracting at n - 1 removes the update from the final requested position.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.wrap_to_zero: Array boundaries do not wrap. A negative mark at zero would alter every reconstructed index.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.sort_then_stop: Difference marks are positional events. Sorting destroys their boundary meaning.

items.prompt: Why are overlapping batch range additions not equivalent to maintaining one moving window?
constraints: All updates are known before reconstruction.
Many update ranges may overlap at one index.

options.text: The difference array superposes start and stop events for many ranges, while one window state represents only one currently maintained interval.
Two mathematical intervals are never allowed to overlap in a window problem.
The difference array works only because update ranges are sorted by delta.
The difference array is equivalent to a window because neither stores any state.

feedback.reason: Difference state represents the combined effects of many intervals through boundary events; a window tracks one evolving interval.
feedback.details: At an index, the active delta may include contributions from any number of overlapping updates. Those contributions are recovered by integrating all event marks. A moving window can overlap a previous window, but its algorithmic state still belongs to one current range and does not encode an arbitrary batch of deferred modifications.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.windows_cannot_overlap: Intervals may overlap in any problem. The distinction is state ownership, not a prohibition on geometric overlap.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.prefix_requires_sorting: The scan follows index order, but values and deltas need not be sorted. Event positions already define the order.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.no_auxiliary_state: A separate difference array stores O(n) boundary state. A window usually stores O(1) or bounded current-state data.

items.prompt: A developer sorts A before applying difference-array updates because they believe prefix accumulation requires sorted input. What happens?
constraints: Each update [l, r] names original positions.
The final result must preserve the original element order.

options.text: The positional ranges now refer to different elements, so the updates are applied to the wrong data.
Sorting changes every O(1) mark into O(n).
Sorting makes negative update deltas invalid.
Sorting automatically removes every r + 1 boundary.

feedback.reason: Difference arrays encode index boundaries in the original domain; sorting changes the ownership of those boundaries.
feedback.details: The algorithm needs a left-to-right index order for reconstruction, but it does not need values to be ordered. Reordering A changes which elements lie inside each update range even though the diff arithmetic still runs. Prefix accumulation and sortedness are separate concepts.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.diff_becomes_quadratic: The asymptotic cost of writing marks does not depend on value order. The failure is semantic, not a complexity change.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.negative_delta_forbidden: Positive and negative deltas are both valid. Sorting has no effect on their algebraic legality.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.stop_mark_disappears: Boundary positions still exist after sorting; they simply refer to the wrong elements for the original problem.

items.prompt: For u inclusive range-add updates on an array of length n, select the total time and extra space of a separate difference array followed by one reconstruction pass.
constraints: Each update writes at most two boundary marks.
The base array is retained separately.

feedback.reason: Each update performs constant endpoint work and one linear prefix scan materializes all final effects; the separate boundary buffer is linear.
feedback.details: Writing u updates costs O(u), and reconstructing n positions costs O(n), so total time is O(u + n). A separate diff buffer uses O(n) extra space. It may be possible to reuse an output buffer or encode changes in place, but the prefix-family optimization does not inherently remove auxiliary memory.

items.prompt: Which variation preserves the boundary-event idea but requires a genuinely two-dimensional encoding?
constraints: Many axis-aligned rectangle additions are applied to a matrix.
Only the final matrix is needed after all updates.

options.text: Use four signed corner marks per rectangle and integrate across both dimensions.
Use only one +delta at the rectangle’s top-left row and one -delta after its bottom row.
Sort all cells and apply the rectangle by value rank.
Maintain one moving rectangle and discard all prior updates.

feedback.reason: A rectangle has independent row and column boundaries, so its start/stop effect must be encoded at signed corners and integrated in two dimensions.
feedback.details: The transfer preserves the same principle—record where an effect begins and ends—but one-dimensional endpoints cannot delimit both axes. Four corner updates apply inclusion-exclusion so a 2D prefix reconstruction activates delta exactly inside the rectangle. Sorting or one current rectangle loses spatial ownership of the batch updates.

wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.one_dimensional_endpoints_only: Row events alone apply the delta across entire rows and cannot restrict the affected columns. Both dimensions need boundaries.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.sort_matrix_values: Rectangle membership is coordinate-based, not value-based. Sorting cells destroys the geometry.
wrongOptionExplanationsByOptionId.single_moving_rectangle: A single active rectangle cannot retain the superposed effects of many deferred updates.
